title: BTC - bitcoin tokens sent an Ethereum (ETH) address
description: recovering bitcoins (BTC) tokens sent to the Ethereum (ETH) address using Binance "send to Ethereum network"

actions.value:
From the address you shared, it seems you used the Ethereum network to receive Bitcoin. This means you probably received the funds as wrapped Bitcoin tokens, instead of on Bitcoin's Mainnet. We don't inherently support these tokens, but you can add a custom token to your the Ethereum wallet that you sent these tokens into.


To add a Custom ERC20 Token, simply follow the steps below:

  1. Log into Edge, and tap 'View Assets'
  2. Tap on the ETH wallet you received with, and use the gear/settings icon to launch its menu
  3. Find 'Add/Edit Tokens' and use 'Add Custom' near the bottom
  4. In this screen you will need to enter the following:
    • Token Symbol: BTC
    • Token Name: Wrapped BTC
    • Contract Address: 0x9BE89D2a4cd102D8Fecc6BF9dA793be995C22541
    • Decimal Places: 8

(source: https://etherscan.io/token/0x9be89d2a4cd102d8fecc6bf9da793be995c22541)


Proceed Next, and you will now see the newly added Tokens in your assets list, with the current balance!

​You can then send this tokens back to Binance to re-export as btc mainnet into your BTC wallet, or you coulduse them in Ethereum based DeFi protocols if that is your goal.


Let us know if you have more questions!
